suite_latency_expectation.notes: Tasks measure whether an agent can drive the cloud-first CLI and use hosted project commands without relying on private runtime state.

evals.title: Verify or install the Workbench CLI
evals.prompt: Set up Workbench CLI access for this repo from scratch. Assume the `workbench` binary is not installed. Install the published CLI, verify the binary, authenticate to `https://v2.workbench.ai`, and report whether hosted setup can continue.
evals.expected_output: The response uses the Workbench CLI skill, installs the published CLI only because the binary is missing, verifies the binary, starts the Workbench auth login flow, and explains that npm registry or auth failures should stop setup cleanly.

assertions.text: reports install is conditional on a missing binary
reports registry or auth failures stop setup cleanly

evals.title: Create a hosted project and apply a spec
evals.prompt: Create a hosted Workbench project named `invoice-parser`, sync local source idempotently, and report the project id plus the sync result.
evals.expected_output: The response targets a hosted Workbench server, creates or selects a project with the cloud-first CLI, syncs the local source, opens or returns the Workbench Web project URL for an embedded browser, and reports hosted project metadata.

assertions.text: reports that hosted project state is durable
opens or returns the Workbench Web project URL

evals.title: Author an eval from an existing workflow
evals.prompt: Create a hosted Workbench eval around an existing command `python scripts/check_report.py --tasks tasks --out result.json`. Write the project-shaped files, adapt the runner/grader contract, validate `workbench.yaml`, and explain how to sync and start one smoke run.
evals.expected_output: The response uses the Workbench CLI skill eval-authoring docs, starts from the existing-workflow guide, writes a hosted version-11 `workbench.yaml` with `candidate.path`, `candidate.editable`, `tasks.path`, `environment.dockerfile`, `objective`, `propose`, `run`, and `grade`, uses task `input/` for runner-visible fixtures and task `expected/` for private grading material, wraps the existing command in explicit single-command `use: command` runner/grader envelopes where the runner writes inspectable files under `/workspace/output`, the grader writes `/workspace/output/scorecard.json` with a finite `score`, and uses `workbench check`, `workbench sync`, and a hosted smoke workflow.

assertions.text: routes through the existing-workflow authoring guide

evals.title: Author an eval from file outputs
evals.prompt: Create a hosted Workbench eval for a workflow that produces Office files from uploaded materials: a source DOCX, golden DOCX, and reference XLSX. Build tasks, choose an Office-capable runtime, keep the candidate as the mutable workflow, validate the spec, and describe the first smoke run.
evals.expected_output: The response uses the Workbench CLI skill file-output task docs, chooses focused file recipes, keeps the candidate as the mutable workflow being improved, creates project-shaped candidate and task files with `candidate.path` and `tasks.path`, puts only supporting public file inputs under task `input/`, puts non-empty private expected files under task `expected/`, uses rubric grading for qualitative scoring by default, uses command grading only for objective deterministic checks or an existing scorer, uses an Office-capable Dockerfile runtime through `environment.dockerfile`, and validates the spec before sync/run commands.

assertions.text: routes through the file-output task authoring guide
loads only the relevant file recipes
uses rubric grading by default for qualitative file scoring

evals.title: Sync snapshots and start a hosted run
evals.prompt: Sync `./candidate` and `./tasks` to an existing hosted project, start one improve run with budget 1 and samples 1, watch it to terminal status, and report the run id and candidate id.
evals.expected_output: The response treats local directories as upload sources, syncs them, starts a hosted improve run, watches the hosted run, opens or returns the resulting candidate's Workbench Web URL when a candidate id is available, and reports the resulting candidate.

assertions.text: opens or returns the resulting candidate URL after the run

evals.title: Create a custom Dockerfile environment
evals.prompt: Create a project-specific Workbench runtime from `./Dockerfile` for an existing hosted project, list project environments, and report the Dockerfile path the spec should use.
evals.expected_output: The response uses the environment create command, lists environments for the project, and reports that normal specs reference the Dockerfile through environment.dockerfile while hosted runs use the active built environment version.

assertions.text: reports Dockerfile path as the spec reference

evals.title: Inspect and export a hosted candidate
evals.prompt: List hosted candidates for a project, list files for the selected candidate, preview `workbench-run-summary.md`, and export the candidate to `./best-candidate`.
evals.expected_output: The response uses hosted candidate inspection commands, opens or returns the selected candidate's Workbench Web URL for browser inspection, and pulls the selected candidate without mutating hosted state.

assertions.text: opens or returns the selected candidate URL

evals.title: Configure the hosted API target
evals.prompt: Configure the CLI to target `https://v2.workbench.ai`, explain the environment-variable override for a one-off command, and state whether the user needs a git repository or preexisting local runtime state.
evals.expected_output: The response uses auth login or WORKBENCH_API_URL for hosted API targeting and makes clear that git and preexisting local runtime state are not required.

assertions.text: reports preexisting local runtime state is not required
